texto: Stanley forced a smile to his lips at the memory of the onesided romance; it was silly, after all, a stupid childhood crush. Who’d fall in love with a fictional character? That was the kind of thing you laughed about as an adult. Or at least Harriet had thought so. He couldn’t quite do it, though. Couldn’t quite see it as a joke. It had felt too real, too raw and wild and fierce, for him todismiss it even now. It was love, of a sort, stunted and unformed as it was. For a time, it had kept him sane.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Amelia Mangan que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Stanley forced a smile to his lips at the memory of the onesided romance; it was silly, after all…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre humor, book, character, com foco em termino e character.

frase_original: Stanley forced a smile to his lips at the memory of the onesided romance; it was silly, after all, a stupid childhood crush. Who’d fall in love with a fictional character? That was the kind of thing you laughed about as an adult. Or at least Harriet had thought so. He couldn’t quite do it, though. Couldn’t quite see it as a joke. It had felt too real, too raw and wild and fierce, for him todismiss it even now. It was love, of a sort, stunted and unformed as it was. For a time, it had kept him sane.

texto: Tthe thing is, Dr. Foster…the truth is, I like Marina.” He eyed the doctor. “And I actually don’t like you very much.”Oh, it was worth it. God, it was worth it. To see the perennially calm face turn pale, only slightly, but still pale; to see him blink away the hurt in his watery, pallid blue eyes.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Amelia Mangan: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Tthe thing is, Dr. Foster…the truth is, I like Marina.” He eyed the doctor. “And I actually don’t…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, appointment, asylum pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de asylum e blue.
